meta_title: NHS Record Access at My Private Clinic Preston | Full Medical History
meta_description: My Private Clinic can access your NHS records with your consent — consultations, blood results, letters, allergies and medications. Private GP in Fulwood, Preston.
hero_tag: A Rare Capability in Private Healthcare
hero_h1: We Can See Your NHS Records. With Your Permission.
hero_sub: Most private clinics start from a blank page. We don't have to. With your consent, we can access your NHS record — so your appointment begins fully informed.
hero_trust: Consent Required · GP Systems Access · Fully Confidential
intro_quote: You've explained your history three times to three different doctors.
intro_heading: NHS Record Access at My Private Clinic, Preston
intro_para_1: Dr Faizal Secretary is able to access your NHS GP records with your consent — including past consultations, blood results, medications, allergies, referral letters and more.
intro_para_2: This means your appointment starts with the full picture. No repeating yourself. No gaps in your history. No starting from scratch.

faqs.question: How does NHS record access work?
faqs.answer: With your consent, Dr Secretary can log in to the NHS GP systems to view your records during your appointment. You control access at all times.

faqs.question: Is this available at other private clinics?
faqs.answer: No — most private GPs do not have access to NHS records. Dr Secretary retains this access as a registered NHS GP, and uses it with your permission to provide better-informed care.

faqs.question: What information can be accessed?
faqs.answer: Past consultations, blood test results, prescribed medications, allergies, referral letters, immunisations and more — the same records your NHS GP can see.

faqs.question: Can I refuse access to my NHS records?
faqs.answer: Of course. Access is always with your explicit consent. You can decline at any point.
